1.title: Endless is my name
1.text: God introduces himself to Moses not by a title of place or nation but by his own nature: "Endless is my name," without beginning of days or end of years — the very theme the book will unfold.

1.question: By what name does God declare himself to Moses (Moses 1:3)?
What did Moses behold when he looked (Moses 1:8)?
options: The world upon which he was created

1.question: How does Moses answer Satan's demand to be worshipped (Moses 1:13)?
options: He asks who Satan is, for Moses is a son of God

1.question: After Satan departs, what fills Moses (Moses 1:24)?
How does Moses describe the number of the inhabitants he beheld (Moses 1:28)?
options: Numberless as the sand upon the sea shore

1.question: By whom did God create the worlds without number (Moses 1:32-33)?
According to Moses 1:39, what is God's work and glory?
options: To bring to pass the immortality and eternal life of man

2.title: And I, God, said
2.text: Unlike Genesis 1, this account is told in the first person — "And I, God, said" — as the Lord narrates the creation directly to Moses.

2.template: Let there be {0}; and there was light

2.question: What did God call the firmament (Moses 2:8)?
By the end of Moses 2:13, which day of creation has closed?
In Moses 2:16, what were the two great lights made to rule?
In Moses 2:20, what did God command the waters to bring forth?
options: The moving creature that hath life, and fowl

2.question: In whose image did God create man (Moses 2:27)?
options: In the image of the beasts
In the image of the angels
In an image of his own devising
In the image of the Only Begotten, male and female

3.title: Created spiritually first
3.text: Moses 3:5 adds a doctrine not stated in Genesis: the Lord created all things spiritually before they were naturally upon the face of the earth.

3.question: On which day did God rest from all his work (Moses 3:2-3)?
In Moses 3:10, a river went out of Eden and parted into how many heads?
For what two purposes did God put the man in the Garden of Eden (Moses 3:15)?
options: To dress it and to keep it

3.template: I will make an {0} for him

3.question: In Moses 3:23, why did Adam say the woman should be called Woman?
options: Because she was taken out of man

4.title: Why Satan fell
4.text: Moses 4 explains what Genesis does not: Satan rebelled because he sought to destroy the agency of man and to take God's own honor and power for himself.

4.question: According to Moses 4:3, why was Satan cast down?
options: Because he sought to destroy the agency of man
Because he refused to be a son
Because he ate of the tree

4.question: In Moses 4:7, through whom did Satan speak to the woman?
In Moses 4:13, after eating, what did Adam and his wife sew together (Moses 4:13)?
Whom did the woman say beguiled her (Moses 4:19)?

4.template: and thou shalt eat the {0} of the field

4.question: What did God do so man would not partake of the tree of life (Moses 4:29-31)?
options: Sent him forth from the Garden of Eden

5.question: In Moses 5:1, how did Adam eat his bread after being driven out?
options: By the sweat of his brow

5.question: In Moses 5:7, of what is the sacrifice a similitude?
options: The rest of the seventh day
The sacrifice of the Only Begotten of the Father

5.title: Eve's joy in redemption
5.text: In Moses 5:11 Eve rejoices that, were it not for their transgression, they never would have had seed nor known the joy of redemption — a striking view of the Fall as a step forward.

5.question: In Moses 5:13, what did men begin to be as they loved Satan more than God?
In Moses 5:17-18, what was Abel's occupation?
In Moses 5:24, what name would Cain be called?
What did Satan tell Cain to swear by (Moses 5:29)?
In Moses 5:34, how did Cain answer the Lord's question about Abel?
In Moses 5:40, what did the Lord set upon Cain?
options: A mark, lest any finding him should kill him

5.question: In Moses 5:43-44, who were Lamech's two wives?
In Moses 5:49-50, why did Lamech slay Irad?
In Moses 5:53-54, why did Lamech's wives declare his secret abroad?
options: They rebelled against him and had not compassion
They were commanded by an angel

5.question: In Moses 5:58, how did the Gospel begin to be preached from the beginning?
options: By the kings of the earth
By holy angels, God's own voice, and the gift of the Holy Ghost

6.question: In Moses 6:5, in whose language was the book of remembrance kept?
In Moses 6:10, what name did Adam give to the son born in his own likeness?
In Moses 6:15, who had great dominion among men in those days?
In Moses 6:21, who did Jared teach in all the ways of God?

6.title: Preachers of righteousness
6.text: Moses 6:23 describes the fathers as preachers of righteousness who called upon all men everywhere to repent — the same message Enoch will soon carry.

6.question: In Moses 6:25, whom did Enoch beget?
In Moses 6:35, what did the Lord tell Enoch to anoint his eyes with?
In Moses 6:40, what was the name of the man who came to Enoch?
In Moses 6:46, what had they written among them?
In Moses 6:51-52, what did the Lord tell Adam he must do to receive the gift of the Holy Ghost?
options: Believe, repent, and be baptized in water

6.question: In Moses 6:54, whose sins cannot be answered upon the heads of the children?
In Moses 6:59-60, by what three things does the Lord teach that men must be born again?
options: Water, the Spirit, and the blood

6.question: In Moses 6:64-65, what happened to Adam after he was laid under the water?
options: He was born of the Spirit and quickened in the inner man

7.question: In Moses 7:3, what happened to Enoch as he stood upon the mount?
options: He was clothed upon with glory

7.question: In Moses 7:8, what came upon the children of Canaan?
In Moses 7:13, what happened when Enoch spake the word of the Lord?
options: The earth trembled and the mountains fled

7.question: In Moses 7:19, what did Enoch call the city that he built?

7.template: the power of {0} was upon all the face of the earth

7.question: In Moses 7:28-29, what astonished Enoch about the God of heaven?
options: That he could not see them

7.question: In Moses 7:33-34, why is the fire of God's indignation kindled against them?
options: Because they are without affection and hate their own blood

7.question: In Moses 7:38, what has the Lord prepared for those who will perish in the floods?
In Moses 7:42-43, whom did Enoch see build an ark?
In Moses 7:48, who cried out in pain because of the wickedness of her children?
options: The earth, the mother of men

7.question: In Moses 7:50-51, what did Enoch plead the Lord would have mercy upon?
In Moses 7:56-57, what did Enoch behold the Son of Man lifted up upon?
In Moses 7:62, what shall the Holy City be called?
In Moses 7:69, why was Zion taken up, so that Zion was not?
options: God received it up into his own bosom
It was hidden in the mountains

8.title: Methuselah, the long-lived
8.text: Moses 8 continues the genealogy toward Noah; Methuselah, whose days would be nine hundred sixty-nine years, was not taken up with Zion so that Noah could come of his line.

8.question: In Moses 8:2, why was Methuselah not taken up (into heaven)?
options: That the covenants of the Lord might be fulfilled, that Noah should be of his loins
Because he hid from the Lord

8.question: In Moses 8:9, what did Lamech say his son Noah would do?
options: Comfort them concerning their work and toil

8.question: In Moses 8:13, what were Noah and his sons called?
In Moses 8:18, who sought to take away Noah's life?
In Moses 8:23-24, what did Noah preach the people must do?
options: Believe, repent, and be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ

8.question: In Moses 8:28-30, with what was the earth filled before God?
